result.first_principles: Theorem R compresses every Euclidean obligation of the chi >= 6 program into one semialgebraic membership: realize the engine F = H-w so that a chosen 3-subset B of N(w) has circumradius exactly 1 (one equation), with the two centers distinct. So W3 is not graph search; it is a question about the moduli space M(F) of unit realizations and the codimension-1 "circumcenter wall" W = {circ_B = 1} inside it. Two provable facts shape everything. (1) Stress floor: H 6-critical forces min degree 5, so for a degree-5 split |E(F)| >= (5n-5)/2 = (2n-3) + (n+1)/2; every realization of every clamp engine carries at least (n+1)/2 independent self-stresses. Clamp engines are necessarily deep in the dependent regime, where realizability itself already demands Euclidean-specific algebraic coincidences (this is exactly how P_510 realizes at +1487 over-determination, L52). (2) Hitting the wall exactly is an intermediate-value event: a connected flex curve whose circ_B values straddle 1 must cross it. That step uses completeness and connectedness of R. Over Q^2 a "flex curve" is totally disconnected, the wall is generically missed, and no chi-5 engine exists at all (chi(Q^2) = 2): the detector passes for the right reason. In L^infty, unit circles have flat arcs, two of them can share a segment, and 3-point cocircularity is not a transversal codim-1 wall: the stratification degenerates, and again no chi-5 engine exists (chi = 4). On R^1 the moduli coordinate (rotation angle) does not exist. The wall-crossing structure therefore lives exactly in the ways R^2 differs from all three controls. The inversion: lineage graphs are isolated, maximally coincident, rigid points of moduli optimized for vertex count, and L57 proved forcing is absent there. If the clamp exists, the two facts above push it onto positive-dimensional strata: paradoxically flexible over-braced engines (hinges, symmetric flexes) whose flex sweeps the circumcenter wall transversally, with the L55-mandated wide interface supplied by wall coincidences rather than by the a priori graph. So the search should sweep continuous moduli and stratify by coincidence corank, not enumerate abstract graphs.

conjectures.name: Paradoxical-Flex Necessity (no rigid clamp)
conjectures.statement: Let G be a unit-distance realizable clamp that is minimal (no proper subgraph is a clamp), with engine F = H-w and clamp realization q per Theorem R (circ_B(q) = 1, s* != t*). Then q lies on a positive-dimensional component of M(F) mod E(2): F admits a nontrivial continuous flex through q, along which circ_B crosses the value 1 transversally. Equivalently: no minimal clamp engine is rigid at its clamp realization; the 'algebraic accident' route (circ_B = 1 at an isolated realization) never occurs for minimal clamps. Supporting lemma (provable now, a VERIFIER target): every degree-5-split clamp engine satisfies |E(F)| >= (5n-5)/2, hence carries >= (n+1)/2 independent self-stresses at every realization, so any such flex is paradoxical (non-generic), forced by symmetry or algebraic dependence.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: (a) Near term: every new chi-5 UDG produced at rigid moduli points, e.g. the wall unions U(theta*) of the experiment below (rigid once the cross edges are added), has ALL non-adjacent pairs FREE under the L45 merge test and L56 add-edge test, extending L57 beyond the lineage. (b) Sharp: any claimed realizable clamp must exhibit rigidity-Jacobian corank >= 4 (3 trivial isometries plus >= 1 flex) at its clamp realization, checkable exactly in sympy. A single rigid clamp falsifies the conjecture outright.
conjectures.why_plausible: The stress-floor lemma puts every clamp engine deep in dependent territory, where an isolated solution of ONE extra equation (circ_B = 1) stacked on (n+1)/2 self-stresses would be an unforced algebraic miracle; a transversal flex crossing is the only robust mechanism for meeting a codim-1 wall. Empirically, 100 percent of known rigid chi-5 realizations (12 graphs, ~2.29M pairs, L57) show zero non-adjacent forcing, and the lineage is rigid by massive over-bracing.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: L52 shows the lineage already realizes via exactly such 'miracles' (P_510 at +1487 over-determination), so the same arithmetic machinery (orbit closures of arccos(5/6)-type rotations) routinely satisfies far more than one surplus equation; an accidental rigid clamp at a deep arithmetic point is not obviously rarer than a paradoxical flex. Also the rigidity-forcing link in the lineage is correlational, not mechanistic, and minimality may fail to control the geometry of q.
conjectures.novelty_claim: The chain '6-criticality (min degree 5) => quantitative stress floor (n+1)/2 => realizable clamps require paradoxical flexibility' has, to my knowledge, never been stated: chromatic criticality and rigidity-matroid dependence are never coupled in the UDG literature. Nearest prior art: this repo's L52/L53 (flex vs codim language, no necessity claim) and symmetry-adapted rigidity theory (Fowler-Guest, Schulze; Dixon's paradoxical K_{3,3} flexes), which never touches chromatic number.

conjectures.name: Mirror normal form for clamp interfaces
conjectures.statement: If any unit-distance realizable clamp exists, then one exists with an exact mirror symmetry sigma: the axis contains both circle centers s* and t*; A = {a, sigma(a)} is a mirror pair; B = {b0, b1, sigma(b1)} with b0 on the axis; the engine F is sigma-invariant; and the clamp flex is the sigma-symmetric flex. In the mirror stratum BOTH load-bearing Theorem-R conditions become robust 1-dimensional sign conditions on the axis: the centers of every circle through {a, sigma(a)} and the circumcenter of B automatically lie ON the axis, so s* != t* is a comparison of two axis coordinates, and circ_B = 1 is a single intermediate-value equation in the symmetric flex parameter. Hence within sigma-symmetric families, clamp configurations form an open (positive-measure) condition, not a measure-zero one.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: (a) Cheap, now: reflective couplings P union sigma(P) of a lineage engine (reflections across lines through vertex pairs) produce coincidence walls of strictly higher mean corank than rotational couplings at matched sample size, because mirror coincidences arrive in pairs plus axis-fixed extras. The experiment below includes both coupling types and measures this directly. (b) The symmetry-extended Maxwell count (character of the rigidity representation, exact in sympy) certifies a symmetric flex in explicitly buildable sigma-invariant over-braced unit frameworks (two mirror copies of a rigid engine joined along axis-fixed vertices) where the scalar count says rigid; failure to find ANY such unit framework up to moderate size falsifies the mechanism. (c) Long term: the first realizable clamp found is mirror-symmetric or symmetrizes to one; an asymmetric clamp with provably no symmetric counterpart falsifies.
conjectures.why_plausible: Symmetry is the canonical generator of paradoxical flexes (Dixon mechanisms, Bottema, the Fowler-Guest/Schulze symmetry-extended Maxwell rule), which Conjecture 1 says are necessary; the mirror stratum is the unique place where Theorem R's two delicate conditions (exact cocircularity, distinct centers) both degrade into stable sign conditions; and the de Grey construction itself emerged from highly symmetric Minkowski machinery, evidence that symmetric strata are where realizable chromatic structure concentrates.
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: sigma-invariance of F induces a coloring involution that typically ENLARGES the space of proper 5-colorings and can destroy forced-difference (forcing wants asymmetry between the s-side and t-side); mirror coincidences may force K4s or unwanted unit pairs, breaking K4-freeness or chi = 5; and the symmetrization step ('one exists implies a symmetric one exists') has no known mechanism and could simply be false even if symmetric clamps are the easiest to find.
conjectures.novelty_claim: No literature connects Schulze-style symmetric flexibility to chromatic-critical UDG construction, and no normal-form statement for forced-pair (clamp) geometry exists anywhere; cocircularity appears in UDG work only as the classical K_{2,3} exclusion. Nearest prior art: symmetric rigidity theory (never chromatic) and de Grey/Polymath16 symmetric constructions (used for vertex economy, never as an interface normal form).

conjectures.name: Corank stratification: coincidence amplification and wall concentration of forcing
conjectures.statement: Fix a realizable chi-5 engine P of the lineage with coordinates in a Q(sqrt3, sqrt11)-type field. On the 3-dimensional moduli of 2-copy unions U(g) = P union g(P), g in E(2), define corank(g) = number of cross pairs (p, g(q)) at distance exactly 1. Then: (i) AMPLIFICATION: corank is unbounded relative to the generic transversality cap of 3 = dim of the moduli; there exist g with corank(g) >= |V(P)|/4, and the locus {corank > 3} is exactly the engine's own arithmetic (rotation angles with cosine in the monoid generated by the construction values 5/6, sqrt(33)/6 and conjugates; translation parts in the difference set P - P), while a height-matched Galois-random g has corank <= 3. (ii) CONCENTRATION: every non-free non-adjacent pair in every U(g), if any exists, occurs at corank(g) >= 4; corank <= 3 unions never force.
conjectures.falsifiable_prediction: (i) Exact enumeration of hinge-rotation walls for the 199-vertex engine S199 finds angles theta with >= 50 simultaneous exact cross unit pairs, all with cos(theta) in the predicted monoid, while random algebraic rotations cap at corank 3; a flat corank distribution falsifies (i). (ii) SAT forcing probes (L45 merge + L56 add-edge) on a sample of corank-1, corank-2, corank-3 walls find zero non-free pairs; a single non-free pair at corank <= 3 falsifies (ii) and is simultaneously the program's missing object or its forced-same dual (win-win falsification).
conjectures.why_plausible: (i) quantifies the coincidence phenomenon that de Grey's construction silently exploits (orbit unions produce massive simultaneous unit coincidences at arithmetic angles); it has never been measured as a distribution. (ii) A corank <= 3 wall attaches at most 3 cross edges between two engines that are individually forcing-free (L57); such a thin interface factors through near-single-vertex ports, and the L55 monoid obstruction confines those to {0, I, J-I, J}, which cannot compose to forced-different on a non-adjacent pair without a forced-same sub-gadget (dead on the lineage by L56).
conjectures.how_it_could_be_wrong: The reduction of a 3-edge interface to the L55 single-port monoid is heuristic: 3 cross edges on 6 distinct boundary vertices form a width-3 interface whose transfer relation lives on color triples, formally outside the monoid; an imprimitive triple relation at corank 3 would falsify (ii). The constants in (i) (|V|/4, the exact monoid description) are educated guesses and may need revision even if the qualitative heavy tail is real.
conjectures.novelty_claim: Treating coincidence corank as a stratification of UDG moduli, conjecturing that color forcing concentrates on deep strata, and predicting the arithmetic support of the high-corank locus are all, as far as I know, unstated: de Grey 2018 and Polymath16 USE deep strata implicitly but never formulate the stratification, the corank statistic, or any forcing-concentration principle. Nearest prior art: de Grey's Minkowski-sum practice; repo L54 wildcard 1 (monotile scaffolds: different mechanism, no corank notion).

experiment.description: Hinge-and-wall sweep on S199. (1) Load the exact coordinates of the 199-vertex lineage engine S199 (same loader as shotD). (2) Form the hinge family U(theta) = P union R_theta(P) (rotation about a chosen vertex; repeat for 2-3 pivot vertices, plus a batch of reflective couplings sigma(P) across lines through vertex pairs for the C2 prediction (a)). Every cross pair gives a wall equation A cos(theta) + B sin(theta) = C, i.e. a quadratic in cos(theta): ~40k candidate wall angles. (3) Cluster the angles numerically (mpmath, 50 digits), then verify the clustered coincidences EXACTLY in sympy (no floating-point acceptance), and record corank(theta) = number of simultaneous exact cross unit pairs; this yields the corank distribution and its arithmetic support (test of C3(i), reflective-vs-rotational comparison for C2(a)). (4) For the top ~20 walls by corank and ~20 random corank-1 to corank-3 walls: build the exact UDG U(theta*) (397 vertices), SAT-check 5-colorability with cadical, then run the shotD probe pair (merge test for forced-different, add-edge test for forced-same) on all cross pairs within graph distance 4 of a new wall edge (~1-2k targeted pairs per wall). Decision rules: any non-free pair at corank <= 3 falsifies C3(ii) (and is a jackpot object); all-free at rigid wall points simultaneously supports C1 prediction (a); the corank distribution decides C3(i); reflective-vs-rotational corank decides C2 prediction (a). One run pays out on all three conjectures.
experiment.repo_feasibility: Everything reuses existing infrastructure: lineage exact coordinates and the L45/L56/L57 probe logic from experiments/combinatorial/shotD_exhaustive_forcing.py; sympy solves each wall equation as a quadratic in cos(theta) over the engine's number field (exact verification only on ~100 clustered candidates, so the exact-arithmetic load is bounded); python-sat (cadical) on 397-vertex graphs is routine (the repo SAT-solves 500-2000 vertex UDGs); no C compiler, no nauty, no new solver needed. Cache under experiments/combinatorial/_cache/wallsweep/.
experiment.est_cost: About one day of coding (mostly harness reuse) plus overnight compute: wall enumeration 1-2 h numeric, exact sympy verification on selected walls 1-3 h, SAT probes ~40 walls x ~1-2k pairs at 0.1-1 s per solve = 6-20 h. Checkpointed and resumable like shotD.

detector_self_check.Q2: The pipeline cannot even start over Q^2: it consumes a chi-5 engine, and chi(Q^2) = 2 (Woodall), so no input exists. Deeper, the mechanism itself is Q^2-blind in the right way: hitting the circumcenter wall exactly is an intermediate-value event on a CONNECTED flex curve, and over Q^2 realization loci are totally disconnected countable sets, so the wall is generically missed; the wall equations A cos(theta) + B sin(theta) = C land in quadratic extensions, outside rational configurations. The abstract clamp does lift to Q^2 but proves nothing there, which is consistent: all chi-relevant content sits in the realization step, exactly where Theorem R put it.
detector_self_check.Linf: Two independent firewalls. First, no chi-5 engine exists in the L^infty plane (chi = 4 exactly, Chilakamarri), so the inverse pipeline has no input. Second, every load-bearing geometric step uses strict convexity and rotational rigidity of the Euclidean circle: 3-point cocircularity as a transversal codim-1 wall, two unit circles meeting in at most 2 points (behind Theorem R's center analysis), and the corank-3 generic cap. In L^infty, unit 'circles' are squares with flat arcs: two of them can share a whole segment, cocircularity is not codim-1, and the wall stratification collapses. The method makes no claim expressible in a general normed plane, so it cannot over-prove chi >= 5 there.
detector_self_check.R1: The search coordinate of the entire program IS the rotation angle theta of O(2) (plus reflective couplings), and the target object (a degree-5 split with a 3-subset at circumradius 1) requires unit spheres carrying >= 3 points. On R^1 the unit sphere is 2 points, maximum UDG degree is 2, circumradius of 3 points is undefined, and there is no continuous rotation: every statement in the proposal is unstatable on the line, so nothing here constrains chi(R) = 2.
